title: WiFi radio (802.11)
text: WiFi (802.11, 2.4 GHz · ESP32-S3) — 17 tools. Scan, capture, and disrupt 2.4 GHz WiFi. The deauth core is built to actually land frames on-air.

title: WiFi > Network scan
text: Network scan (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Lists nearby WiFi networks and the clients on them. How it works: Monitor-mode capture of 802.11 beacon and probe frames → SSID, BSSID, channel, RSSI, encryption.

title: WiFi > Deauth
text: Deauth (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Kicks devices off a network (one you control) to force reconnects. How it works: Spoofs 802.11 deauth/disassoc management frames for the AP↔client pair with correct sequence counters; sniffs associated clients first so it targets real stations.

title: WiFi > PMKID + 4-way handshake
text: PMKID + 4-way handshake (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Grabs the crypto material needed to crack a WiFi password offline. How it works: Captures the PMKID from EAPOL message 1, or the full 4-way handshake, and saves it in hashcat 22000 format on SD.

title: WiFi > Evil Portal
text: Evil Portal (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Stands up a fake login page to see what users would type. How it works: SoftAP + catch-all DNS + HTTP server serving a captive credential page; submissions logged to SD.

title: WiFi > Karma
text: Karma (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Answers devices hunting for their saved networks. How it works: Replies to client probe requests for any SSID, impersonating remembered APs so devices auto-associate.

title: WiFi > Beacon spam
text: Beacon spam (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Fills the air with fake network names. How it works: Broadcasts many crafted beacon frames with varied SSIDs/BSSIDs/channels.

title: WiFi > Probe sniff
text: Probe sniff (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Reveals which networks nearby phones are looking for. How it works: Captures 802.11 probe-request frames and logs the SSIDs clients leak.

title: WiFi > Evil Twin
text: Evil Twin (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Clones a real AP to lure its clients. How it works: Copies a target's SSID/security shell on a SoftAP, optionally paired with deauth to push clients over.

title: WiFi > Wardrive + GPS
text: Wardrive + GPS (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: Maps WiFi networks as you walk or drive. How it works: Logs APs with GNSS coordinates to SD as Wigle-style CSV.

title: WiFi > Argus — handshake-hunter gotchi
text: Argus — handshake-hunter gotchi (a WiFi / 802.11 tool). What it does: An autonomous pet that hunts WiFi handshakes for you. How it works: RL channel-picker hops bands, fires deauths and captures handshakes unattended; mood sprite reflects hunt state; SD-persisted. Modes: HUNT / STEALTH / SURGICAL / STORM.

title: Bluetooth LE radio (BLE)
text: Bluetooth LE (BLE, ESP32-S3 · NimBLE) — 15 tools. Identify, spam, clone, and probe BLE — including a real CVE check.

title: Bluetooth LE > Classifying scan
text: Classifying scan (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Lists nearby Bluetooth devices and what they actually are. How it works: Parses BLE advertisements and matches OUI, Apple Continuity, and Google Fast Pair databases to name the device type.

title: Bluetooth LE > Spam (Apple/Samsung/Google/Windows)
text: Spam (Apple/Samsung/Google/Windows) (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Pops fake pairing popups on nearby phones and PCs. How it works: Broadcasts vendor advertising payloads (Continuity / Fast Pair / Swift Pair) that trigger the OS pairing UI. Cooperative-tick scheduled so every nearby device sees it.

title: Bluetooth LE > Sour Apple (CVE-2023-42941)
text: Sour Apple (CVE-2023-42941) (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Floods nearby iPhones with pairing prompts to lock them up. How it works: Emits malformed Apple proximity-pairing adverts that overload iOS BLE handling.

title: Bluetooth LE > WhisperPair probe (CVE-2025-36911)
text: WhisperPair probe (CVE-2025-36911) (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Checks Fast Pair earbuds/speakers for a takeover bug. How it works: Sends a real Key-Based Pairing write (secp256r1 ECDH + AES-128-ECB) to in-use Fast Pair accessories; a response when not in pairing mode = vulnerable, and the hidden BR/EDR MAC can be lifted.

title: Bluetooth LE > Bad-KB HID
text: Bad-KB HID (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Acts as a Bluetooth keyboard to type into a paired host. How it works: Emulates a BLE HID keyboard and streams keystroke reports (DuckyScript-style).

title: Bluetooth LE > Tracker detect
text: Tracker detect (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Finds AirTags/Tiles that might be following you. How it works: Scans for known tracker advertisement signatures and flags ones that persist around you.

title: Bluetooth LE > GATT explorer
text: GATT explorer (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Browses what a BLE device exposes. How it works: Connects and enumerates GATT services/characteristics for read/write.

title: Bluetooth LE > Find-My emulator
text: Find-My emulator (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Makes the device look like an Apple Find My beacon. How it works: Broadcasts Find My-format advertisements.

title: Bluetooth LE > Clone
text: Clone (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Mimics a target BLE device's advertisement. How it works: Captures and re-broadcasts the advertising payload/address.

title: Bluetooth LE > Flood
text: Flood (a Bluetooth LE / BLE tool). What it does: Saturates an area with BLE advertisements. How it works: High-rate advertising across many randomized addresses.

title: Sub-GHz radio (CC1101)
text: Sub-GHz (CC1101, CC1101 · 300–928 MHz) — 10 tools. Capture, decode, and replay garage/car/sensor remotes, plus a big signal library.

title: Sub-GHz > Capture + protocol decoder
text: Capture + protocol decoder (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Records a remote and tells you which protocol it is. How it works: CC1101 demodulates OOK/FSK; the pulse train is timed and decoded for fixed-code protocols (Princeton PT2262, CAME, NICE, Linear).

title: Sub-GHz > RAW record (RMT)
text: RAW record (RMT) (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Records any sub-GHz signal exactly, even unknown ones. How it works: Captures edge timings via the ESP32 RMT peripheral for low-jitter RAW pulses.

title: Sub-GHz > Replay .sub
text: Replay .sub (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Re-transmits a recorded signal; Flipper-compatible. How it works: Reads Flipper .sub pulse data and transmits it on the captured frequency/modulation (armed-gated).

title: Sub-GHz > Signal library (3,190+)
text: Signal library (3,190+) (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: A built-in catalog of ready-to-send signals. How it works: Bundled .sub files organized by category (cars, garages, gas-station, etc.).

title: Sub-GHz > Spectrum analyzer
text: Spectrum analyzer (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Shows which frequencies are active right now. How it works: Sweeps RSSI across channels and plots a live waterfall / peak-hold / radar scope.

title: Sub-GHz > Brute force
text: Brute force (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Tries every code for simple fixed-code remotes. How it works: Iterates a de-Bruijn keyspace of fixed codes and transmits each.

title: Sub-GHz > Jammer
text: Jammer (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Blocks a sub-GHz frequency. How it works: Continuous-carrier (CW) transmit on the target frequency. Illegal in most places — see Legal.

title: Sub-GHz > Jam detect
text: Jam detect (a Sub-GHz / CC1101 tool). What it does: Warns you when a band is being jammed. How it works: Monitors the RSSI floor for sustained abnormal energy.

title: 2.4 GHz radio (nRF24)
text: 2.4 GHz (nRF24, nRF24L01+) — 6 tools. Sniff and inject Nordic Enhanced ShockBurst — wireless mice/keyboards and more.

title: 2.4 GHz > Promiscuous ESB sniffer
text: Promiscuous ESB sniffer (a 2.4 GHz / nRF24 tool). What it does: Sniffs 2.4 GHz wireless keyboards, mice, and other Nordic gear. How it works: Uses the Goodspeed pseudo-promiscuous trick (illegal address + short preamble) to capture Enhanced ShockBurst frames.

title: 2.4 GHz > MouseJack injection
text: MouseJack injection (a 2.4 GHz / nRF24 tool). What it does: Injects keystrokes into vulnerable wireless mice/keyboards. How it works: Targets unauthenticated Nordic dongles by injecting crafted ESB HID keystroke frames.

title: 2.4 GHz > nRF24 BLE spam
text: nRF24 BLE spam (a 2.4 GHz / nRF24 tool). What it does: Sends BLE advertisements from a cheap nRF24 radio. How it works: Hand-builds BLE-compatible packets with manual CRC24 + whitening on advertising channels 37/38/39.

title: 2.4 GHz > Spectrum analyzer
text: Spectrum analyzer (a 2.4 GHz / nRF24 tool). What it does: Visualizes 2.4 GHz channel activity. How it works: Reads received-power-detector across channels for an energy map.

title: 2.4 GHz > CW jammer
text: CW jammer (a 2.4 GHz / nRF24 tool). What it does: Blocks a 2.4 GHz channel. How it works: Constant-carrier output on a chosen channel. Illegal in most places — see Legal.

title: LoRa & Mesh radio (SX1262)
text: LoRa & Mesh (SX1262, SX1262) — 8 tools. Long-range LoRa scan/TX, GNSS, and a full Meshtastic leaf node.

title: LoRa & Mesh > Multi-band scan
text: Multi-band scan (a LoRa & Mesh / SX1262 tool). What it does: Listens for LoRa traffic on 433 / 868 / 915 MHz. How it works: Configures SX1262 spreading-factor/bandwidth/coding-rate and logs packets with RSSI/SNR.

title: LoRa & Mesh > Beacon TX + GPS
text: Beacon TX + GPS (a LoRa & Mesh / SX1262 tool). What it does: Broadcasts your position over LoRa. How it works: Transmits a LoRa frame carrying GNSS coordinates.

title: LoRa & Mesh > Meshtastic leaf node
text: Meshtastic leaf node (a LoRa & Mesh / SX1262 tool). What it does: Join and chat on a Meshtastic mesh from your pocket. How it works: Implements the LongFast preset + protocol: chat, node list, paging, and position.

title: LoRa & Mesh > LoRa analyzer
text: LoRa analyzer (a LoRa & Mesh / SX1262 tool). What it does: Live capture and inspection of LoRa packets. How it works: Streams decoded headers/payloads as they arrive.

title: LoRa & Mesh > GPS fix
text: GPS fix (a LoRa & Mesh / SX1262 tool). What it does: Gets a location lock for wardrive/beacon. How it works: Parses NMEA from the GNSS module with automatic baud detection.

title: LAN / Network radio (RJ45 / IP)
text: LAN / Network (RJ45 / IP, wired + SaltyJack) — 18 tools. A full wired/wireless LAN suite, including the SaltyJack credential arsenal — no PC or Pi needed.

title: LAN / Network > Port scan
text: Port scan (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Finds open ports on a host or across the LAN. How it works: TCP connect scan over the wired (W5500) or WiFi link.

title: LAN / Network > Ping / DNS
text: Ping / DNS (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Basic reachability and name-lookup checks. How it works: ICMP echo + DNS resolution.

title: LAN / Network > SSDP poisoner
text: SSDP poisoner (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Interferes with UPnP device discovery. How it works: Spoofs SSDP responses to discovery queries.

title: LAN / Network > Honeypot
text: Honeypot (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Fake service that catches whoever's scanning. How it works: Opens listening ports and logs connection attempts.

title: LAN / Network > Reverse TCP tunnel
text: Reverse TCP tunnel (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Phone-home channel for remote access. How it works: Outbound TCP to a listener you control.

title: LAN / Network > UART shell
text: UART shell (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Serial command console to the device. How it works: Interactive shell over USB-CDC / UART.

title: LAN / Network > SaltyJack — DHCP starvation
text: SaltyJack — DHCP starvation (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Drains a network's pool of IP addresses. How it works: Floods DHCP Discover/Request with random MACs until exhaustion (auto-detects on NAK ≥ 20).

title: LAN / Network > SaltyJack — Rogue DHCP
text: SaltyJack — Rogue DHCP (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Hands clients poisoned network settings. How it works: Races the real server (STA) or serves DHCP on our SoftAP (AP), setting attacker gateway + DNS for MitM.

title: LAN / Network > SaltyJack — Responder
text: SaltyJack — Responder (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Captures Windows logins on the LAN. How it works: LLMNR + NBT-NS name-poisoning plus SMB NTLMv2 challenge; hashes saved in hashcat-5600 format.

title: LAN / Network > SaltyJack — WPAD NTLM
text: SaltyJack — WPAD NTLM (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Tricks Windows into handing over proxy creds. How it works: Serves a PAC file on :80 + HTTP 407 Proxy-Authenticate; domain clients auto-authenticate.

title: LAN / Network > SaltyJack — on-device cracker
text: SaltyJack — on-device cracker (a LAN / Network / RJ45 / IP tool). What it does: Cracks captured NTLMv2 hashes on the device itself. How it works: Pure-C HMAC-MD5 wordlist runner over hashcat-5600 lines — no PC tether.

title: IR · BadUSB · System radio (IR / USB)
text: IR · BadUSB · System (IR / USB, ESP32-S3) — 12 tools. HID payloads, IR remotes, and the pocket utilities that make it a daily-carry.

title: IR · BadUSB · System > USB-HID payload runner (BadUSB)
text: USB-HID payload runner (BadUSB) (a IR · BadUSB · System / IR / USB tool). What it does: Types a scripted payload into any computer. How it works: Enumerates as a USB HID keyboard over native USB-C and runs DuckyScript.

title: IR · BadUSB · System > TV-B-Gone
text: TV-B-Gone (a IR · BadUSB · System / IR / USB tool). What it does: Powers off most TVs in range. How it works: Blasts a database of TV power IR codes.

title: IR · BadUSB · System > Samsung remote
text: Samsung remote (a IR · BadUSB · System / IR / USB tool). What it does: Control Samsung TVs. How it works: Sends the Samsung IR keymap (active-HIGH LED, verified vs Flipper-IRDB).

title: IR · BadUSB · System > MAC randomizer
text: MAC randomizer (a IR · BadUSB · System / IR / USB tool). What it does: Changes the device's WiFi MAC. How it works: Sets a consumer-OUI random MAC for OPSEC.

title: IR · BadUSB · System > Utilities
text: Utilities (a IR · BadUSB · System / IR / USB tool). What it does: Flashlight, stopwatch, calculator, morse, SD format, file browser. How it works: Standalone pocket tools.

title: IR · BadUSB · System > Theme engine
text: Theme engine (a IR · BadUSB · System / IR / USB tool). What it does: Six swappable color schemes. How it works: POSEIDON / PHANTOM / MATRIX / AMBER / E-INK / TRON, persisted to NVS, with ambient procedural motion + idle screensavers.

title: Recon & Defense radio (multi-radio)
text: Recon & Defense (multi-radio, HUNT · multi-radio) — 4 tools. One proximity-hunting submenu that homes in on an emitter across any radio, geiger-style.

title: Recon & Defense > BLE tracker hunt
text: BLE tracker hunt (a Recon & Defense / multi-radio tool). What it does: Hot/cold homing on a chosen BLE device. How it works: Maps RSSI to a proximity meter that beeps faster as you close in.

title: Recon & Defense > Passive tracker scan
text: Passive tracker scan (a Recon & Defense / multi-radio tool). What it does: Finds trackers around you without engaging them. How it works: Listens for tracker advert signatures and ranks by signal.

title: Recon & Defense > CC1101 finder
text: CC1101 finder (a Recon & Defense / multi-radio tool). What it does: Homes in on a sub-GHz emitter. How it works: Geiger-style RSSI tracking on a chosen frequency.

title: Recon & Defense > nRF24 finder
text: nRF24 finder (a Recon & Defense / multi-radio tool). What it does: Homes in on a 2.4 GHz Nordic emitter. How it works: Geiger-style RSSI tracking; the HUNT menu switches the radio to match the target automatically.

title: TRIDENT radio (ESP32-C5)
text: TRIDENT (ESP32-C5, ESP32-C5 satellite · 5 GHz) — satellite. A plug-in C5 node over ESP-NOW that does the 5 GHz / 802.15.4 work the S3 can't.

title: TRIDENT > 5 GHz deauth
text: 5 GHz deauth (a TRIDENT / ESP32-C5 tool). What it does: Deauth on 5 GHz networks (the Cardputer's S3 can't reach 5 GHz). How it works: C5 raw 802.11 frames with a binary patch bypassing ieee80211_raw_frame_sanity_check ; targeted + broadcast.

title: TRIDENT > 802.15.4 Zigbee sniffer
text: 802.15.4 Zigbee sniffer (a TRIDENT / ESP32-C5 tool). What it does: Captures Zigbee / Thread radio traffic. How it works: C5 802.15.4 PHY capture.

title: TRIDENT > 5 GHz PMKID
text: 5 GHz PMKID (a TRIDENT / ESP32-C5 tool). What it does: Handshake capture on 5 GHz networks. How it works: PMKID / EAPOL capture on the C5 radio.

title: TRIDENT > Dual-band scan
text: Dual-band scan (a TRIDENT / ESP32-C5 tool). What it does: Lists 2.4 + 5 GHz networks with their security. How it works: Scans both bands and red-flags WPA3; results stream to POSEIDON over ESP-NOW (auto-pairs on channel 1).

title: 👁️ Meet Argus
text: ## 👁️ Meet Argus > They blinded the hundred-eyed giant by killing him. POSEIDON just gave him a new way to see. **Argus Panoptes** was the all-seeing sentinel of myth — a hundred eyes, never all closed at once, cursed to keep watch for eternity. POSEIDON carries the last of those eyes. He's a **cursed Argonian demigod** chained into the deck, his hundred eyes melted down into a single antenna, all that watching-hunger pointed at the airwaves around you. He channel-hops on instinct, learns where the prey gathers, and snares WiFi handshakes out of thin air while your Cardputer sits in your pocket — tireless, because the curse won't *let* him rest. The moods that cross his face are the curse showing through. ---

title: 🌊 New to all this? Start here
text: ## 🌊 New to all this? Start here POSEIDON is a **pocket hacking gadget**. It runs on a tiny handheld with a real keyboard — the M5Stack Cardputer — and turns it into a Swiss-army knife for the invisible signals all around you. **No PC, no coding.** Flash it once and start typing. **What can it actually do?** - 📶 **WiFi** — see every network around you, test your own, map them as you walk - 📱 **Bluetooth** — find lost trackers (AirTags / Tiles), spot hidden ones following you, poke at nearby gadgets - 🚗 **Remotes & signals** — record and replay garage doors, car fobs, doorbells, smart plugs *(on stuff you own)* - 📺 **Infrared** — a universal TV remote, plus the classic "turn off every TV in the room" button - 🐾 **Argus** — your on-screen pet that hunts WiFi on its own and reacts with moods - 🎨 **Six looks** — flip the whole interface between six themes, matrix-green to vaporwave **Get it on your device (~2 minutes):** 1. Grab the free **M5Burner** app, *or* open the [**web installer**](https://generaldussduss.github.io/poseidon/install.html) in Chrome. 2. Search **POSEIDON** (or hit Install), plug in your Cardputer, press the button. 3. Done — it boots straight to the menu. Just start typing. > ⚠️ **Be cool.** This is for exploring *your own* stuff and learning how wireless actually works. Don't touch networks or devices you don't own — that's both uncool and illegal. <div align

title: What is this?
text: ## What is this? POSEIDON is keyboard-first pentesting firmware for the **M5Stack Cardputer-Advance** (ESP32-S3, real QWERTY keyboard + 240x135 color screen). **163 features** span WiFi, Bluetooth LE, sub-GHz, 2.4 GHz, LoRa & Mesh, Infrared, LAN/network, BadUSB, and recon — all reachable by typing. It's in the same genre as Flipper Zero, Bruce, Evil-M5Project, and ESP32Marauder, but the keyboard changes everything: - **Letter-mnemonic menus** — every item has a hotkey, so you jump straight to it - **Typed parameters** — enter a channel, an SSID, a frequency directly, no thumb-stick scrolling - **Six swappable themes** + a procedural ambient motion layer painted behind every menu - **Argus** — a 96x96 mood-sprite gotchi that hunts handshakes on its own while you browse the menu Runs standalone on the Cardputer-Advance. Optional snap-on satellites extend the radio range: - **TRIDENT** — ESP32-C5 node over ESP-NOW: 5 GHz deauth, Zigbee/802.15.4 sniff, PMKID - **nRF52840 Feather** — BLE 5.0 features (proof-of-concept today, hardened in v0.7)

title: Quick Start — Flash it
text: ## Quick Start — Flash it Two ways onto the Cardputer-Advance, both browser-based, no toolchain. Pick one.

title: 1. Cardputer-Advance
text: ### 1. Cardputer-Advance **A) M5Burner — one-click, easiest.** Open [M5Burner](https://docs.m5stack.com/en/download), search **POSEIDON**, hit **Burn**. Done. **B) Web flasher** (Chrome / Edge / Opera). Plug the Cardputer in over USB-C and open: > **<https://generaldussduss.github.io/poseidon/install.html>** Click **Connect**, pick the serial port, **Install** — the page flashes the current factory image at offset 0x0 for you.

title: 2. TRIDENT satellite (ESP32-C5)
text: ### 2. TRIDENT satellite (ESP32-C5) The C5 isn't in M5Burner's chip list, so it flashes with the Espressif **esptool-js** web flasher over WebSerial. 1. Plug the C5 board into USB and open the Espressif flasher: **<https://espressif.github.io/esptool-js/>** (Chrome / Edge) 2. **Connect** and select the C5's serial port 3. Add the three TRIDENT bins from the [latest release](https://github.com/GeneralDussDuss/poseidon/releases/latest) at these offsets: | Offset | File | |---|---| | 0x2000 | bootloader | | 0x8000 | partition table | | 0x10000 | TRIDENT application | 4. **Program**. When it finishes, power-cycle the C5 — it pairs to POSEIDON over ESP-NOW automatically. > A one-click guided version of these steps also lives at **<https://generaldussduss.github.io/poseidon/flash/>**.

title: Build from source
text: ### Build from source bash

title: Cardputer (ESP32-S3) — PlatformIO
text: # Cardputer (ESP32-S3) — PlatformIO git clone https://github.com/GeneralDussDuss/poseidon.git cd poseidon pio run -t upload  The TRIDENT C5 firmware builds with **ESP-IDF** (idf.py build flash) — see the trident/ directory for its README.

title: Signature Features
text: ## Signature Features **Argus — the cursed all-seeing one.** A hundred-eyed demigod bound into the deck (lore up top ↑), Argus is an autonomous gotchi with a 96x96 mood sprite — twelve portraits (Watching, Pleased, Annoyed, Calculating, Old Fury, Sleeping…), each one the curse showing through as it reacts in real time to what it's catching. Under the cute face is a reinforcement-learning channel picker that learns which channels pay off and persists its brain to SD. Leave POSEIDON in your pocket and Argus keeps hunting handshakes across HUNT / STEALTH / SURGICAL / STORM modes. **TRIDENT — snap on a second band.** A pocket-sized ESP32-C5 satellite that pairs to POSEIDON over ESP-NOW and adds the radios the S3 can't do alone: **5 GHz deauth**, **Zigbee / 802.15.4 sniffing**, and **PMKID** capture. Flash it once with the web flasher, power it up, and it auto-pairs — your Cardputer stays the keyboard, TRIDENT becomes the antenna. **SaltyJack — a pocket LAN attack arsenal.** Drop POSEIDON onto a network (WiFi STA or wired W5500) and run the kind of attacks that usually need a laptop and a Pi: **DHCP starvation**, **rogue DHCP**, **Responder** (LLMNR/NBT-NS → NTLM), **WPAD** and **Autodiscover** credential capture, plus an **on-device NTLMv2 cracker**. A faithful port that proudly credits [@7h30th3r0n3](https://github.com/7h30th3r0n3)'s RaspyJack and Evil-M5Project — go star both. **

title: POSEIDON C5 Node
text: # POSEIDON C5 Node Companion firmware for the ESP32-C5. The C5 is the only Espressif chip with: - **5 GHz WiFi** (2.4 + 5, 802.11ax) - **802.15.4** (Zigbee / Thread) This firmware turns a C5 into a remote radio for POSEIDON (running on the Cardputer's S3). Communication happens over ESP-NOW — the C5 listens for command frames from POSEIDON, executes the requested scan or attack on its native radio, and streams results back.

title: Protocol (over PigSync ESP-NOW)
text: ## Protocol (over PigSync ESP-NOW)  struct posei_msg_t { uint32_t magic; // 0x504F5345 "POSE" uint8_t version; // 3 uint8_t type; // see POSEI_TYPE_* uint16_t seq; uint8_t payload[230]; uint8_t payload_len; } __packed;  Types: | Type | Name | Direction | Payload | |------|------|-----------|---------| | 1 | HELLO | both | node name, heap, gps | | 10 | CMD_PING | S3→C5 | — | | 11 | CMD_SCAN_5G | S3→C5 | duration_ms | | 12 | CMD_SCAN_ZB | S3→C5 | channel or 0xFF for hop | | 13 | CMD_SCAN_2G | S3→C5 | duration_ms | | 14 | CMD_DEAUTH_5G | S3→C5 | bssid + channel | | 15 | CMD_STOP | S3→C5 | — | | 20 | RESP_PONG | C5→S3 | — | | 21 | RESP_AP_BATCH | C5→S3 | count + array of APs | | 22 | RESP_ZB_FRAME | C5→S3 | pan id + src + dst | | 23 | RESP_STATUS | C5→S3 | last_cmd + ok flag |

title: Building
text: ## Building Requires ESP-IDF v5.2 or newer (first version with C5 support). This firmware builds against v5.5.4. bash . $IDF_PATH/export.sh cd c5_node idf.py set-target esp32c5 idf.py build idf.py -p /dev/ttyUSB0 flash monitor

title: Status
text: ## Status Shipped and hardware-verified (protocol **v3**). Implements HELLO/PING, 5 GHz scan, **5 GHz deauth**, **PMKID capture**, and **Zigbee / 802.15.4 sniff**, all driven from POSEIDON over ESP-NOW, plus 2.4 GHz scan and GPS passthrough. Known limitation: full 4-way handshake capture over ESP-NOW is constrained by the 230-byte payload (large EAPOL frames must be fragmented) — 5 GHz scan, PMKID, and deauth are unaffected.
